[[Image:Descendants of Izanagi and Izanami.svg|thumb|right|250px|Myth of the Birth of the Gods (Deities Born from Izanagi and Izanami) [[Media:Descendants of Izanagi and Izanami.svg|View in SVG (for supported browsers)]]]] '''Katakurokushin''' (家宅六神) refers to the collective name of the six [[deities (Shinto)|deities]] representing (or protecting) [[households]] in [[Shinto]].
The Katakurokushin represent the materials and structure of buildings. According to the ''[[Kojiki]]'', all six deities were born after the [[Kuniumi]] (birth of the land), following the birth of '''{{ill|Ōkoto-oshi'o|ja|大事忍男神}}''', as children of {{ill|Izanagi|ja|イザナギ}} and {{ill|Izanami|ja|イザナミ}}.
== Iwatsuchi-biko-no-Kami == The first deity born is '''{{ill|Iwatsuchi-BiKo|ja|石土毘古神}}'''.
== Iwasu-hime-no-Kami == The second deity is '''{{ill|Iwasu-HiMe|ja|石巣比売神}}'''.
According to the {{ill|Koshiden|ja|古史伝}}, these two deities are alternative names for {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|ja|上筒男神|Sumiyoshi sanjin=Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Uwatatsu-no-Kami}}. In {{ill|Jinmyōkō|8=ja|9=神名考}}, it is stated that Iwatsuchi-biko-no-Kami governs over the [[earth]], while Iwasu-hime-no-Kami governs over [[:en:Sand|sand]].
== Ōtohiwake-no-Kami == The third deity is '''{{ill|Ōtohi-WaKe|ja|大戸日別神}}''', whose gender is unknown. The ''[[Kojikiden]]'' suggests that this deity may be confused with {{ill|Ōna-obi|ja|大直毘神}}. In ''Jinmyōkō'', Ōtohiwake-no-Kami is considered one of the gate deities.
== Ame-no-Fukio-no-Kami == The fourth deity is '''{{ill|Ame-no-FukiO|ja|天之吹男神}}'''. The ''Kojikiden'' interprets this deity, based on the name, as being the same as {{ill|Ibukidōnushi|ja|気吹戸主}}, who appears in the ''[[Oharae no Kotoba|Ōharae no Kotoba]]''. In ''Jinmyōkō'', this deity is considered the deity of rooftops.
== Ōya-biko-no-Kami == The fifth deity is '''{{ill|Ōya-biko|ja|大屋毘古神}}'''. This is considered a different deity from the {{ill|Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|ja|大屋毘古神|lt=Ōya-biko-no-Kami}} who appears in the myths of {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主}} and is also an alternative name for {{ill|Itonokami|ja|五十猛神}}.
== Kazamo-tsuwake-no-Oshio-no-Kami == The last deity born is '''{{ill|Kazamo-Tsuwake-no-OshiO|ja|風木津別之忍男神}}'''. Regarding the reading of the name, the original text notes that the character for "木" should be read as "mo." However, the ''Kojikiden'' suggests this annotation was mistakenly inserted later, proposing the reading "Kazaketsuwake Oshio." The ''Kojikiden'' also considers this deity to be the same as {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|ja|底筒男神|en|Sumiyoshi sanjin=Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Sokotsutsuo-no-Kami}} or {{ill|Hayasasurahime|ja|速佐須良比売}}, who appears in the ''Ōharae no Kotoba''.

[[zh:%E5%AE%B6%E5%AE%85%E5%85%AD%E7%A5%9E]] [[ja:Katakurokushin]]

[[Image:Descendants of Izanagi and Izanami.svg|thumb|right|250px|Myth of the Birth of the Gods (Deities Born from Izanagi and Izanami) [[Media:Descendants of Izanagi and Izanami.svg|View in SVG (for supported browsers)]]]] '''Katakurokushin''' (家宅六神) refers to the collective name of the six [[deities (Shinto)|deities]] representing (or protecting) [[households]] in [[Shinto]].
The Katakurokushin represent the materials and structure of buildings. According to the ''[[Kojiki]]'', all six deities were born after the [[Kuniumi]] (birth of the land), following the birth of '''{{ill|Ōkoto-oshi'o|ja|大事忍男神}}''', as children of {{ill|Izanagi|ja|イザナギ}} and {{ill|Izanami|ja|イザナミ}}.
== Iwatsuchi-biko-no-Kami == The first deity born is '''{{ill|Iwatsuchi-BiKo|ja|石土毘古神}}'''.
== Iwasu-hime-no-Kami == The second deity is '''{{ill|Iwasu-HiMe|ja|石巣比売神}}'''.
According to the {{ill|Koshiden|ja|古史伝}}, these two deities are alternative names for {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|ja|上筒男神|Sumiyoshi sanjin=Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Uwatatsu-no-Kami}}. In {{ill|Jinmyōkō|8=ja|9=神名考}}, it is stated that Iwatsuchi-biko-no-Kami governs over the [[earth]], while Iwasu-hime-no-Kami governs over [[:en:Sand|sand]].
== Ōtohiwake-no-Kami == The third deity is '''{{ill|Ōtohi-WaKe|ja|大戸日別神}}''', whose gender is unknown. The ''[[Kojikiden]]'' suggests that this deity may be confused with {{ill|Ōna-obi|ja|大直毘神}}. In ''Jinmyōkō'', Ōtohiwake-no-Kami is considered one of the gate deities.
== Ame-no-Fukio-no-Kami == The fourth deity is '''{{ill|Ame-no-FukiO|ja|天之吹男神}}'''. The ''Kojikiden'' interprets this deity, based on the name, as being the same as {{ill|Ibukidōnushi|ja|気吹戸主}}, who appears in the ''[[Oharae no Kotoba|Ōharae no Kotoba]]''. In ''Jinmyōkō'', this deity is considered the deity of rooftops.
== Ōya-biko-no-Kami == The fifth deity is '''{{ill|Ōya-biko|ja|大屋毘古神}}'''. This is considered a different deity from the {{ill|Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|ja|大屋毘古神|lt=Ōya-biko-no-Kami}} who appears in the myths of {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主}} and is also an alternative name for {{ill|Itonokami|ja|五十猛神}}.
== Kazamo-tsuwake-no-Oshio-no-Kami == The last deity born is '''{{ill|Kazamo-Tsuwake-no-OshiO|ja|風木津別之忍男神}}'''. Regarding the reading of the name, the original text notes that the character for "木" should be read as "mo." However, the ''Kojikiden'' suggests this annotation was mistakenly inserted later, proposing the reading "Kazaketsuwake Oshio." The ''Kojikiden'' also considers this deity to be the same as {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|ja|底筒男神|en|Sumiyoshi sanjin=Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Sokotsutsuo-no-Kami}} or {{ill|Hayasasurahime|ja|速佐須良比売}}, who appears in the ''Ōharae no Kotoba''.
== See Also == * [[List of Japanese deities]] * [[Kamiumi]]

[[Image:Descendants of Izanagi and Izanami.svg|thumb|right|250px|Myth of the Birth of the Gods (Deities Born from Izanagi and Izanami) [[Media:Descendants of Izanagi and Izanami.svg|View in SVG (for supported browsers)]]]] '''Katakurokushin''' (家宅六神) refers to the collective name of the six [[deities (Shinto)|deities]] representing (or protecting) [[households]] in [[Shinto]].
The Katakurokushin represent the materials and structure of buildings. According to the ''[[Kojiki]]'', all six deities were born after the [[Kuniumi]] (birth of the land), following the birth of '''{{ill|Ōkoto-oshi'o|ja|大事忍男神}}''', as children of {{ill|Izanagi|ja|イザナギ}} and {{ill|Izanami|ja|イザナミ}}.
== Iwatsuchi-biko-no-Kami == The first deity born is '''{{ill|Iwatsuchi-BiKo|ja|石土毘古神}}'''.
== Iwasu-hime-no-Kami == The second deity is '''{{ill|Iwasu-HiMe|ja|石巣比売神}}'''.
According to the {{ill|Koshiden|ja|古史伝}}, these two deities are alternative names for {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|ja|上筒男神|Sumiyoshi sanjin=Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Uwatatsu-no-Kami}}. In {{ill|Jinmyōkō|8=ja|9=神名考}}, it is stated that Iwatsuchi-biko-no-Kami governs over the [[earth]], while Iwasu-hime-no-Kami governs over [[:en:Sand|sand]].
== Ōtohiwake-no-Kami == The third deity is '''{{ill|Ōtohi-WaKe|ja|大戸日別神}}''', whose gender is unknown. The ''[[Kojikiden]]'' suggests that this deity may be confused with {{ill|Ōna-obi|ja|大直毘神}}. In ''Jinmyōkō'', Ōtohiwake-no-Kami is considered one of the gate deities.
== Ame-no-Fukio-no-Kami == The fourth deity is '''{{ill|Ame-no-FukiO|ja|天之吹男神}}'''. The ''Kojikiden'' interprets this deity, based on the name, as being the same as {{ill|Ibukidōnushi|ja|気吹戸主}}, who appears in the ''[[Oharae no Kotoba|Ōharae no Kotoba]]''. In ''Jinmyōkō'', this deity is considered the deity of rooftops.
== Ōya-biko-no-Kami == The fifth deity is '''{{ill|Ōya-biko|ja|大屋毘古神}}'''. This is considered a different deity from the {{ill|Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|ja|大屋毘古神|lt=Ōya-biko-no-Kami}} who appears in the myths of {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主}} and is also an alternative name for {{ill|Itonokami|ja|五十猛神}}.
== Kazamo-tsuwake-no-Oshio-no-Kami == The last deity born is '''{{ill|Kazamo-Tsuwake-no-OshiO|ja|風木津別之忍男神}}'''. Regarding the reading of the name, the original text notes that the character for "木" should be read as "mo." However, the ''Kojikiden'' suggests this annotation was mistakenly inserted later, proposing the reading "Kazaketsuwake Oshio." The ''Kojikiden'' also considers this deity to be the same as {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|ja|底筒男神|en|Sumiyoshi sanjin=Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Sokotsutsuo-no-Kami}} or {{ill|Hayasasurahime|ja|速佐須良比売}}, who appears in the ''Ōharae no Kotoba''.
== See Also == * [[List of Japanese deities]] * [[Kamiumi]]

[[Image:Descendants of Izanagi and Izanami.svg|thumb|right|250px|Myth of the Birth of the Gods (Deities Born from Izanagi and Izanami) [[Media:Descendants of Izanagi and Izanami.svg|View in SVG (for supported browsers)]]]] '''Katakurokushin''' (家宅六神) refers to the collective name of the six [[deities (Shinto)|deities]] representing (or protecting) [[households]] in [[Shinto]].
The Katakurokushin represent the materials and structure of buildings. According to the ''[[Kojiki]]'', all six deities were born after the [[Kuniumi]] (birth of the land), following the birth of '''{{ill|Ōkoto-oshi'o|ja|大事忍男神}}''', as children of {{ill|Izanagi|ja|イザナギ}} and {{ill|Izanami|ja|イザナミ}}.
== Iwatsuchi-biko-no-Kami == The first deity born is '''{{ill|Iwatsuchi-BiKo|ja|石土毘古神}}'''.
== Iwasu-hime-no-Kami == The second deity is '''{{ill|Iwasu-HiMe|ja|石巣比売神}}'''.
According to the {{ill|Koshiden|ja|古史伝}}, these two deities are alternative names for {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|ja|上筒男神|Sumiyoshi sanjin=Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Uwatatsu-no-Kami}}. In {{ill|Jinmyōkō|8=ja|9=神名考}}, it is stated that Iwatsuchi-biko-no-Kami governs over the [[earth]], while Iwasu-hime-no-Kami governs over [[:en:Sand|sand]].
== Ōtohiwake-no-Kami == The third deity is '''{{ill|Ōtohi-WaKe|ja|大戸日別神}}''', whose gender is unknown. The ''[[Kojikiden]]'' suggests that this deity may be confused with {{ill|Ōna-obi|ja|大直毘神}}. In ''Jinmyōkō'', Ōtohiwake-no-Kami is considered one of the gate deities.
== Ame-no-Fukio-no-Kami == The fourth deity is '''{{ill|Ame-no-FukiO|ja|天之吹男神}}'''. The ''Kojikiden'' interprets this deity, based on the name, as being the same as {{ill|Ibukidōnushi|ja|気吹戸主}}, who appears in the ''[[Oharae no Kotoba|Ōharae no Kotoba]]''. In ''Jinmyōkō'', this deity is considered the deity of rooftops.
== Ōya-biko-no-Kami == The fifth deity is '''{{ill|Ōya-biko|ja|大屋毘古神}}'''. This is considered a different deity from the {{ill|Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|ja|大屋毘古神|lt=Ōya-biko-no-Kami}} who appears in the myths of {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主}} and is also an alternative name for {{ill|Itonokami|ja|五十猛神}}.
== Kazamo-tsuwake-no-Oshio-no-Kami == The last deity born is '''{{ill|Kazamo-Tsuwake-no-OshiO|ja|風木津別之忍男神}}'''. Regarding the reading of the name, the original text notes that the character for "木" should be read as "mo." However, the ''Kojikiden'' suggests this annotation was mistakenly inserted later, proposing the reading "Kazaketsuwake Oshio." The ''Kojikiden'' also considers this deity to be the same as {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|ja|底筒男神|en|Sumiyoshi sanjin=Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Sokotsutsuo-no-Kami}} or {{ill|Hayasasurahime|ja|速佐須良比売}}, who appears in the ''Ōharae no Kotoba''.

[[Image:Descendants of Izanagi and Izanami.svg|thumb|right|250px|Myth of the Birth of the Gods (Deities Born from Izanagi and Izanami) [[Media:Descendants of Izanagi and Izanami.svg|View in SVG (for supported browsers)]]]] '''Katakurokushin''' (家宅六神) refers to the collective name of the six [[deities (Shinto)|deities]] representing (or protecting) [[households]] in [[Shinto]].
The Katakurokushin represent the materials and structure of buildings. According to the ''[[Kojiki]]'', all six deities were born after the [[Kuniumi]] (birth of the land), following the birth of '''{{ill|Ōkoto-oshi'o|ja|大事忍男神}}''', as children of {{ill|Izanagi|ja|イザナギ}} and {{ill|Izanami|ja|イザナミ}}.
== Iwatsuchi-biko-no-Kami == The first deity born is '''{{ill|Iwatsuchi-BiKo|ja|石土毘古神}}'''.
== Iwasu-hime-no-Kami == The second deity is '''{{ill|Iwasu-HiMe|ja|石巣比売神}}'''.
According to the {{ill|Koshiden|ja|古史伝}}, these two deities are alternative names for {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|ja|上筒男神|Sumiyoshi sanjin=Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Uwatatsu-no-Kami}}. In {{ill|Jinmyōkō|8=ja|9=神名考}}, it is stated that Iwatsuchi-biko-no-Kami governs over the [[earth]], while Iwasu-hime-no-Kami governs over [[:en:Sand|sand]].
== Ōtohiwake-no-Kami == The third deity is '''{{ill|Ōtohi-WaKe|ja|大戸日別神}}''', whose gender is unknown. The ''[[Kojikiden]]'' suggests that this deity may be confused with {{ill|Ōna-obi|ja|大直毘神}}. In ''Jinmyōkō'', Ōtohiwake-no-Kami is considered one of the gate deities.
== Ame-no-Fukio-no-Kami == The fourth deity is '''{{ill|Ame-no-FukiO|ja|天之吹男神}}'''. The ''Kojikiden'' interprets this deity, based on the name, as being the same as {{ill|Ibukidōnushi|ja|気吹戸主}}, who appears in the ''[[Oharae no Kotoba|Ōharae no Kotoba]]''. In ''Jinmyōkō'', this deity is considered the deity of rooftops.
== Ōya-biko-no-Kami == The fifth deity is '''{{ill|Ōya-biko|ja|大屋毘古神}}'''. This is considered a different deity from the {{ill|Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|ja|大屋毘古神|lt=Ōya-biko-no-Kami}} who appears in the myths of {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主}} and is also an alternative name for {{ill|Itonokami|ja|五十猛神}}.
== Kazamo-tsuwake-no-Oshio-no-Kami == The last deity born is '''{{ill|Kazamo-Tsuwake-no-OshiO|ja|風木津別之忍男神}}'''. Regarding the reading of the name, the original text notes that the character for "木" should be read as "mo." However, the ''Kojikiden'' suggests this annotation was mistakenly inserted later, proposing the reading "Kazaketsuwake Oshio." The ''Kojikiden'' also considers this deity to be the same as {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|ja|底筒男神|en|Sumiyoshi sanjin=Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Sokotsutsuo-no-Kami}} or {{ill|Hayasasurahime|ja|速佐須良比売}}, who appears in the ''Ōharae no Kotoba''.
== See Also == * [[List of Japanese deities]] * [[Kamiumi]]

revision.text:
[[Image:Descendants of Izanagi and Izanami.svg|thumb|right|250px|Myth of the Birth of the Gods (Deities Born from Izanagi and Izanami) [[Media:Descendants of Izanagi and Izanami.svg|View in SVG (for supported browsers)]]]] '''Katakurokushin''' (家宅六神) refers to the collective name of the six [[deities (Shinto)|deities]] representing (or protecting) [[households]] in [[Shinto]].
The Katakurokushin represent the materials and structure of buildings. According to the ''[[Kojiki]]'', all six deities were born after the [[Kuniumi]] (birth of the land), following the birth of '''{{ill|Ōkoto-oshi'o|ja|大事忍男神}}''', as children of {{ill|Izanagi|ja|イザナギ}} and {{ill|Izanami|ja|イザナミ}}.
== Iwatsuchi-biko-no-Kami == The first deity born is '''{{ill|Iwatsuchi-BiKo|ja|石土毘古神}}'''.
== Iwasu-hime-no-Kami == The second deity is '''{{ill|Iwasu-HiMe|ja|石巣比売神}}'''.
According to the {{ill|Koshiden|ja|古史伝}}, these two deities are alternative names for {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|ja|上筒男神|Sumiyoshi sanjin=Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Uwatatsu-no-Kami}}. In {{ill|Jinmyōkō|8=ja|9=神名考}}, it is stated that Iwatsuchi-biko-no-Kami governs over the [[earth]], while Iwasu-hime-no-Kami governs over [[:en:Sand|sand]].
== Ōtohiwake-no-Kami == The third deity is '''{{ill|Ōtohi-WaKe|ja|大戸日別神}}''', whose gender is unknown. The ''[[Kojikiden]]'' suggests that this deity may be confused with {{ill|Ōna-obi|ja|大直毘神}}. In ''Jinmyōkō'', Ōtohiwake-no-Kami is considered one of the gate deities.
== Ame-no-Fukio-no-Kami == The fourth deity is '''{{ill|Ame-no-FukiO|ja|天之吹男神}}'''. The ''Kojikiden'' interprets this deity, based on the name, as being the same as {{ill|Ibukidōnushi|ja|気吹戸主}}, who appears in the ''[[Oharae no Kotoba|Ōharae no Kotoba]]''. In ''Jinmyōkō'', this deity is considered the deity of rooftops.
== Ōya-biko-no-Kami == The fifth deity is '''{{ill|Ōya-biko|ja|大屋毘古神}}'''. This is considered a different deity from the {{ill|Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|ja|大屋毘古神|lt=Ōya-biko-no-Kami}} who appears in the myths of {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主}} and is also an alternative name for {{ill|Itonokami|ja|五十猛神}}.
== Kazamo-tsuwake-no-Oshio-no-Kami == The last deity born is '''{{ill|Kazamo-Tsuwake-no-OshiO|ja|風木津別之忍男神}}'''. Regarding the reading of the name, the original text notes that the character for "木" should be read as "mo." However, the ''Kojikiden'' suggests this annotation was mistakenly inserted later, proposing the reading "Kazaketsuwake Oshio." The ''Kojikiden'' also considers this deity to be the same as {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|ja|底筒男神|en|Sumiyoshi sanjin=Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Sokotsutsuo-no-Kami}} or {{ill|Hayasasurahime|ja|速佐須良比売}}, who appears in the ''Ōharae no Kotoba''.
== See Also == * [[List of Japanese deities]] * [[Kamiumi]]

[[Image:Descendants of Izanagi and Izanami.svg|thumb|right|250px|Myth of the Birth of the Gods (Deities Born from Izanagi and Izanami) [[Media:Descendants of Izanagi and Izanami.svg|View in SVG (for supported browsers)]]]] '''Katakurokushin''' (家宅六神) refers to the collective name of the six [[deities (Shinto)|deities]] representing (or protecting) [[households]] in [[Shinto]].
The Katakurokushin represent the materials and structure of buildings. According to the ''[[Kojiki]]'', all six deities were born after the [[Kuniumi]] (birth of the land), following the birth of '''{{ill|Ōkoto-oshi'o|ja|大事忍男神}}''', as children of {{ill|Izanagi|ja|イザナギ}} and {{ill|Izanami|ja|イザナミ}}.
== Iwatsuchi-biko-no-Kami == The first deity born is '''{{ill|Iwatsuchi-BiKo|ja|石土毘古神}}'''.
== Iwasu-hime-no-Kami == The second deity is '''{{ill|Iwasu-HiMe|ja|石巣比売神}}'''.
According to the {{ill|Koshiden|ja|古史伝}}, these two deities are alternative names for {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|ja|上筒男神|Sumiyoshi sanjin=Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Uwatatsu-no-Kami}}. In {{ill|Jinmyōkō|8=ja|9=神名考}}, it is stated that Iwatsuchi-biko-no-Kami governs over the [[earth]], while Iwasu-hime-no-Kami governs over [[:en:Sand|sand]].
== Ōtohiwake-no-Kami == The third deity is '''{{ill|Ōtohi-WaKe|ja|大戸日別神}}''', whose gender is unknown. The ''[[Kojikiden]]'' suggests that this deity may be confused with {{ill|Ōna-obi|ja|大直毘神}}. In ''Jinmyōkō'', Ōtohiwake-no-Kami is considered one of the gate deities.
== Ame-no-Fukio-no-Kami == The fourth deity is '''{{ill|Ame-no-FukiO|ja|天之吹男神}}'''. The ''Kojikiden'' interprets this deity, based on the name, as being the same as {{ill|Ibukidōnushi|ja|気吹戸主}}, who appears in the ''[[Oharae no Kotoba|Ōharae no Kotoba]]''. In ''Jinmyōkō'', this deity is considered the deity of rooftops.
== Ōya-biko-no-Kami == The fifth deity is '''{{ill|Ōya-biko|ja|大屋毘古神}}'''. This is considered a different deity from the {{ill|Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|ja|大屋毘古神|lt=Ōya-biko-no-Kami}} who appears in the myths of {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主}} and is also an alternative name for {{ill|Itonokami|ja|五十猛神}}.
== Kazamo-tsuwake-no-Oshio-no-Kami == The last deity born is '''{{ill|Kazamo-Tsuwake-no-OshiO|ja|風木津別之忍男神}}'''. Regarding the reading of the name, the original text notes that the character for "木" should be read as "mo." However, the ''Kojikiden'' suggests this annotation was mistakenly inserted later, proposing the reading "Kazaketsuwake Oshio." The ''Kojikiden'' also considers this deity to be the same as {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|ja|底筒男神|en|Sumiyoshi sanjin=Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Sokotsutsuo-no-Kami}} or {{ill|Hayasasurahime|ja|速佐須良比売}}, who appears in the ''Ōharae no Kotoba''.

[[Image:Descendants of Izanagi and Izanami.svg|thumb|right|250px|Myth of the Birth of the Gods (Deities Born from Izanagi and Izanami) [[Media:Descendants of Izanagi and Izanami.svg|View in SVG (for supported browsers)]]]] '''Katakurokushin''' (家宅六神) refers to the collective name of the six [[deities (Shinto)|deities]] representing (or protecting) [[households]] in [[Shinto]].
The Katakurokushin represent the materials and structure of buildings. According to the ''[[Kojiki]]'', all six deities were born after the [[Kuniumi]] (birth of the land), following the birth of '''{{ill|Ōkoto-oshi'o|ja|大事忍男神}}''', as children of {{ill|Izanagi|ja|イザナギ}} and {{ill|Izanami|ja|イザナミ}}.
== Iwatsuchi-biko-no-Kami == The first deity born is '''{{ill|Iwatsuchi-BiKo|ja|石土毘古神}}'''.
== Iwasu-hime-no-Kami == The second deity is '''{{ill|Iwasu-HiMe|ja|石巣比売神}}'''.
According to the {{ill|Koshiden|ja|古史伝}}, these two deities are alternative names for {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|ja|上筒男神|Sumiyoshi sanjin=Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Uwatatsu-no-Kami}}. In {{ill|Jinmyōkō|8=ja|9=神名考}}, it is stated that Iwatsuchi-biko-no-Kami governs over the [[earth]], while Iwasu-hime-no-Kami governs over [[:en:Sand|sand]].
== Ōtohiwake-no-Kami == The third deity is '''{{ill|Ōtohi-WaKe|ja|大戸日別神}}''', whose gender is unknown. The ''[[Kojikiden]]'' suggests that this deity may be confused with {{ill|Ōna-obi|ja|大直毘神}}. In ''Jinmyōkō'', Ōtohiwake-no-Kami is considered one of the gate deities.
== Ame-no-Fukio-no-Kami == The fourth deity is '''{{ill|Ame-no-FukiO|ja|天之吹男神}}'''. The ''Kojikiden'' interprets this deity, based on the name, as being the same as {{ill|Ibukidōnushi|ja|気吹戸主}}, who appears in the ''[[Oharae no Kotoba|Ōharae no Kotoba]]''. In ''Jinmyōkō'', this deity is considered the deity of rooftops.
== Ōya-biko-no-Kami == The fifth deity is '''{{ill|Ōya-biko|ja|大屋毘古神}}'''. This is considered a different deity from the {{ill|Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|ja|大屋毘古神|lt=Ōya-biko-no-Kami}} who appears in the myths of {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主}} and is also an alternative name for {{ill|Itonokami|ja|五十猛神}}.
== Kazamo-tsuwake-no-Oshio-no-Kami == The last deity born is '''{{ill|Kazamo-Tsuwake-no-OshiO|ja|風木津別之忍男神}}'''. Regarding the reading of the name, the original text notes that the character for "木" should be read as "mo." However, the ''Kojikiden'' suggests this annotation was mistakenly inserted later, proposing the reading "Kazaketsuwake Oshio." The ''Kojikiden'' also considers this deity to be the same as {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|ja|底筒男神|en|Sumiyoshi sanjin=Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Sokotsutsuo-no-Kami}} or {{ill|Hayasasurahime|ja|速佐須良比売}}, who appears in the ''Ōharae no Kotoba''.

[[Image:Descendants of Izanagi and Izanami.svg|thumb|right|250px|Myth of the Birth of the Gods (Deities Born from Izanagi and Izanami) [[Media:Descendants of Izanagi and Izanami.svg|View in SVG (for supported browsers)]]]] '''Katakurokushin''' (家宅六神) refers to the collective name of the six [[deities (Shinto)|deities]] representing (or protecting) [[households]] in [[Shinto]].
The Katakurokushin represent the materials and structure of buildings. According to the ''[[Kojiki]]'', all six deities were born after the [[Kuniumi]] (birth of the land), following the birth of '''{{ill|Ōkoto-oshi'o|ja|大事忍男神}}''', as children of {{ill|Izanagi|ja|イザナギ}} and {{ill|Izanami|ja|イザナミ}}.
== Iwatsuchi-biko-no-Kami == The first deity born is '''{{ill|Iwatsuchi-BiKo|ja|石土毘古神}}'''.
== Iwasu-hime-no-Kami == The second deity is '''{{ill|Iwasu-HiMe|ja|石巣比売神}}'''.
According to the {{ill|Koshiden|ja|古史伝}}, these two deities are alternative names for {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|ja|上筒男神|Sumiyoshi sanjin=Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Uwatatsu-no-Kami}}. In {{ill|Jinmyōkō|8=ja|9=神名考}}, it is stated that Iwatsuchi-biko-no-Kami governs over the [[earth]], while Iwasu-hime-no-Kami governs over [[:en:Sand|sand]].
== Ōtohiwake-no-Kami == The third deity is '''{{ill|Ōtohi-WaKe|ja|大戸日別神}}''', whose gender is unknown. The ''[[Kojikiden]]'' suggests that this deity may be confused with {{ill|Ōna-obi|ja|大直毘神}}. In ''Jinmyōkō'', Ōtohiwake-no-Kami is considered one of the gate deities.
== Ame-no-Fukio-no-Kami == The fourth deity is '''{{ill|Ame-no-FukiO|ja|天之吹男神}}'''. The ''Kojikiden'' interprets this deity, based on the name, as being the same as {{ill|Ibukidōnushi|ja|気吹戸主}}, who appears in the ''[[Oharae no Kotoba|Ōharae no Kotoba]]''. In ''Jinmyōkō'', this deity is considered the deity of rooftops.
== Ōya-biko-no-Kami == The fifth deity is '''{{ill|Ōya-biko|ja|大屋毘古神}}'''. This is considered a different deity from the {{ill|Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|ja|大屋毘古神|lt=Ōya-biko-no-Kami}} who appears in the myths of {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主}} and is also an alternative name for {{ill|Itonokami|ja|五十猛神}}.
== Kazamo-tsuwake-no-Oshio-no-Kami == The last deity born is '''{{ill|Kazamo-Tsuwake-no-OshiO|ja|風木津別之忍男神}}'''. Regarding the reading of the name, the original text notes that the character for "木" should be read as "mo." However, the ''Kojikiden'' suggests this annotation was mistakenly inserted later, proposing the reading "Kazaketsuwake Oshio." The ''Kojikiden'' also considers this deity to be the same as {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|ja|底筒男神|en|Sumiyoshi sanjin=Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Sokotsutsuo-no-Kami}} or {{ill|Hayasasurahime|ja|速佐須良比売}}, who appears in the ''Ōharae no Kotoba''.

The ''Kojikiden'' interprets this deity, based on the name, as being the same as {{ill|Ibukidōnushi|ja|気吹戸主}}, who appears in the ''[[Oharae no Kotoba|Ōharae no Kotoba]]''. In ''Jinmyōkō'', this deity is considered the deity of rooftops.
== Ōya-biko-no-Kami == The fifth deity is '''{{ill|Ōya-biko|ja|大屋毘古神}}'''. This is considered a different deity from the {{ill|Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|ja|大屋毘古神|lt=Ōya-biko-no-Kami}} who appears in the myths of {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主}} and is also an alternative name for {{ill|Itonokami|ja|五十猛神}}.
== Kazamo-tsuwake-no-Oshio-no-Kami == The last deity born is '''{{ill|Kazamo-Tsuwake-no-OshiO|ja|風木津別之忍男神}}'''. Regarding the reading of the name, the original text notes that the character for "木" should be read as "mo." However, the ''Kojikiden'' suggests this annotation was mistakenly inserted later, proposing the reading "Kazaketsuwake Oshio." The ''Kojikiden'' also considers this deity to be the same as {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|ja|底筒男神|en|Sumiyoshi sanjin=Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Sokotsutsuo-no-Kami}} or {{ill|Hayasasurahime|ja|速佐須良比売}}, who appears in the ''Ōharae no Kotoba''.
== See Also == * [[List of Japanese deities]] * [[Kamiumi]]

revision.text:
[[Image:Descendants of Izanagi and Izanami.svg|thumb|right|250px|Myth of the Birth of the Gods (Deities Born from Izanagi and Izanami) [[Media:Descendants of Izanagi and Izanami.svg|View in SVG (for supported browsers)]]]] '''Katakurokushin''' (家宅六神) refers to the collective name of the six [[deities (Shinto)|deities]] representing (or protecting) [[households]] in [[Shinto]].
The Katakurokushin represent the materials and structure of buildings. According to the ''[[Kojiki]]'', all six deities were born after the [[Kuniumi]] (birth of the land), following the birth of '''{{ill|Ōkoto-oshi'o|ja|大事忍男神}}''', as children of {{ill|Izanagi|ja|イザナギ}} and {{ill|Izanami|ja|イザナミ}}.
== Iwatsuchi-biko-no-Kami == The first deity born is '''{{ill|Iwatsuchi-BiKo|ja|石土毘古神}}'''.
== Iwasu-hime-no-Kami == The second deity is '''{{ill|Iwasu-HiMe|ja|石巣比売神}}'''.
According to the {{ill|Koshiden|ja|古史伝}}, these two deities are alternative names for {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|ja|上筒男神|Sumiyoshi sanjin=Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Uwatatsu-no-Kami}}. In {{ill|Jinmyōkō|8=ja|9=神名考}}, it is stated that Iwatsuchi-biko-no-Kami governs over the [[earth]], while Iwasu-hime-no-Kami governs over [[:en:Sand|sand]].
== Ōtohiwake-no-Kami == The third deity is '''{{ill|Ōtohi-WaKe|ja|大戸日別神}}''', whose gender is unknown. The ''[[Kojikiden]]'' suggests that this deity may be confused with {{ill|Ōna-obi|ja|大直毘神}}. In ''Jinmyōkō'', Ōtohiwake-no-Kami is considered one of the gate deities.
== Ame-no-Fukio-no-Kami == The fourth deity is '''{{ill|Ame-no-FukiO|ja|天之吹男神}}'''. The ''Kojikiden'' interprets this deity, based on the name, as being the same as {{ill|Ibukidōnushi|ja|気吹戸主}}, who appears in the ''[[Oharae no Kotoba|Ōharae no Kotoba]]''. In ''Jinmyōkō'', this deity is considered the deity of rooftops.
== Ōya-biko-no-Kami == The fifth deity is '''{{ill|Ōya-biko|ja|大屋毘古神}}'''. This is considered a different deity from the {{ill|Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|ja|大屋毘古神|lt=Ōya-biko-no-Kami}} who appears in the myths of {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主}} and is also an alternative name for {{ill|Itonokami|ja|五十猛神}}.
== Kazamo-tsuwake-no-Oshio-no-Kami == The last deity born is '''{{ill|Kazamo-Tsuwake-no-OshiO|ja|風木津別之忍男神}}'''. Regarding the reading of the name, the original text notes that the character for "木" should be read as "mo." However, the ''Kojikiden'' suggests this annotation was mistakenly inserted later, proposing the reading "Kazaketsuwake Oshio." The ''Kojikiden'' also considers this deity to be the same as {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|ja|底筒男神|en|Sumiyoshi sanjin=Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Sokotsutsuo-no-Kami}} or {{ill|Hayasasurahime|ja|速佐須良比売}}, who appears in the ''Ōharae no Kotoba''.
== See Also == * [[List of Japanese deities]] * [[Kamiumi]]

According to the ''[[Kojiki]]'', all six deities were born after the [[Kuniumi]] (birth of the land), following the birth of '''{{ill|Ōkoto-oshi'o|ja|大事忍男神}}''', as children of {{ill|Izanagi|ja|イザナギ}} and {{ill|Izanami|ja|イザナミ}}.

== Iwasu-hime-no-Kami == The second deity is '''{{ill|Iwasu-HiMe|ja|石巣比売神}}'''.
According to the {{ill|Koshiden|ja|古史伝}}, these two deities are alternative names for {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|ja|上筒男神|Sumiyoshi sanjin=Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Uwatatsu-no-Kami}}. In {{ill|Jinmyōkō|8=ja|9=神名考}}, it is stated that Iwatsuchi-biko-no-Kami governs over the [[earth]], while Iwasu-hime-no-Kami governs over [[:en:Sand|sand]].
== Ōtohiwake-no-Kami == The third deity is '''{{ill|Ōtohi-WaKe|ja|大戸日別神}}''', whose gender is unknown. The ''[[Kojikiden]]'' suggests that this deity may be confused with {{ill|Ōna-obi|ja|大直毘神}}. In ''Jinmyōkō'', Ōtohiwake-no-Kami is considered one of the gate deities.
== Ame-no-Fukio-no-Kami == The fourth deity is '''{{ill|Ame-no-FukiO|ja|天之吹男神}}'''. The ''Kojikiden'' interprets this deity, based on the name, as being the same as {{ill|Ibukidōnushi|ja|気吹戸主}}, who appears in the ''[[Oharae no Kotoba|Ōharae no Kotoba]]''. In ''Jinmyōkō'', this deity is considered the deity of rooftops.
== Ōya-biko-no-Kami == The fifth deity is '''{{ill|Ōya-biko|ja|大屋毘古神}}'''. This is considered a different deity from the {{ill|Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|ja|大屋毘古神|lt=Ōya-biko-no-Kami}} who appears in the myths of {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主}} and is also an alternative name for {{ill|Itonokami|ja|五十猛神}}.
== Kazamo-tsuwake-no-Oshio-no-Kami == The last deity born is '''{{ill|Kazamo-Tsuwake-no-OshiO|ja|風木津別之忍男神}}'''. Regarding the reading of the name, the original text notes that the character for "木" should be read as "mo." However, the ''Kojikiden'' suggests this annotation was mistakenly inserted later, proposing the reading "Kazaketsuwake Oshio." The ''Kojikiden'' also considers this deity to be the same as {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|ja|底筒男神|en|Sumiyoshi sanjin=Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Sokotsutsuo-no-Kami}} or {{ill|Hayasasurahime|ja|速佐須良比売}}, who appears in the ''Ōharae no Kotoba''.

[[Image:Descendants of Izanagi and Izanami.svg|thumb|right|250px|Myth of the Birth of the Gods (Deities Born from Izanagi and Izanami) [[Media:Descendants of Izanagi and Izanami.svg|View in SVG (for supported browsers)]]]] '''Katakurokushin''' (家宅六神) refers to the collective name of the six [[deities (Shinto)|deities]] representing (or protecting) [[households]] in [[Shinto]].
The Katakurokushin represent the materials and structure of buildings. According to the ''[[Kojiki]]'', all six deities were born after the [[Kuniumi]] (birth of the land), following the birth of '''{{ill|Ōkoto-oshi'o|ja|大事忍男神}}''', as children of {{ill|Izanagi|ja|イザナギ}} and {{ill|Izanami|ja|イザナミ}}.
== Iwatsuchi-biko-no-Kami == The first deity born is '''{{ill|Iwatsuchi-BiKo|ja|石土毘古神}}'''.
== Iwasu-hime-no-Kami == The second deity is '''{{ill|Iwasu-HiMe|ja|石巣比売神}}'''.
According to the {{ill|Koshiden|ja|古史伝}}, these two deities are alternative names for {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|ja|上筒男神|Sumiyoshi sanjin=Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Uwatatsu-no-Kami}}. In {{ill|Jinmyōkō|8=ja|9=神名考}}, it is stated that Iwatsuchi-biko-no-Kami governs over the [[earth]], while Iwasu-hime-no-Kami governs over [[:en:Sand|sand]].
== Ōtohiwake-no-Kami == The third deity is '''{{ill|Ōtohi-WaKe|ja|大戸日別神}}''', whose gender is unknown. The ''[[Kojikiden]]'' suggests that this deity may be confused with {{ill|Ōna-obi|ja|大直毘神}}. In ''Jinmyōkō'', Ōtohiwake-no-Kami is considered one of the gate deities.
== Ame-no-Fukio-no-Kami == The fourth deity is '''{{ill|Ame-no-FukiO|ja|天之吹男神}}'''. The ''Kojikiden'' interprets this deity, based on the name, as being the same as {{ill|Ibukidōnushi|ja|気吹戸主}}, who appears in the ''[[Oharae no Kotoba|Ōharae no Kotoba]]''. In ''Jinmyōkō'', this deity is considered the deity of rooftops.
== Ōya-biko-no-Kami == The fifth deity is '''{{ill|Ōya-biko|ja|大屋毘古神}}'''. This is considered a different deity from the {{ill|Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|ja|大屋毘古神|lt=Ōya-biko-no-Kami}} who appears in the myths of {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主}} and is also an alternative name for {{ill|Itonokami|ja|五十猛神}}.
== Kazamo-tsuwake-no-Oshio-no-Kami == The last deity born is '''{{ill|Kazamo-Tsuwake-no-OshiO|ja|風木津別之忍男神}}'''. Regarding the reading of the name, the original text notes that the character for "木" should be read as "mo." However, the ''Kojikiden'' suggests this annotation was mistakenly inserted later, proposing the reading "Kazaketsuwake Oshio." The ''Kojikiden'' also considers this deity to be the same as {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|ja|底筒男神|en|Sumiyoshi sanjin=Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Sokotsutsuo-no-Kami}} or {{ill|Hayasasurahime|ja|速佐須良比売}}, who appears in the ''Ōharae no Kotoba''.

The ''Kojikiden'' interprets this deity, based on the name, as being the same as {{ill|Ibukidōnushi|ja|気吹戸主}}, who appears in the ''[[Oharae no Kotoba|Ōharae no Kotoba]]''. In ''Jinmyōkō'', this deity is considered the deity of rooftops.
== Ōya-biko-no-Kami == The fifth deity is '''{{ill|Ōya-biko|ja|大屋毘古神}}'''. This is considered a different deity from the {{ill|Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|ja|大屋毘古神|lt=Ōya-biko-no-Kami}} who appears in the myths of {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主}} and is also an alternative name for {{ill|Itonokami|ja|五十猛神}}.
== Kazamo-tsuwake-no-Oshio-no-Kami == The last deity born is '''{{ill|Kazamo-Tsuwake-no-OshiO|ja|風木津別之忍男神}}'''. Regarding the reading of the name, the original text notes that the character for "木" should be read as "mo." However, the ''Kojikiden'' suggests this annotation was mistakenly inserted later, proposing the reading "Kazaketsuwake Oshio." The ''Kojikiden'' also considers this deity to be the same as {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|ja|底筒男神|en|Sumiyoshi sanjin=Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Sokotsutsuo-no-Kami}} or {{ill|Hayasasurahime|ja|速佐須良比売}}, who appears in the ''Ōharae no Kotoba''.

[[Image:Descendants of Izanagi and Izanami.svg|thumb|right|250px|Myth of the Birth of the Gods (Deities Born from Izanagi and Izanami) [[Media:Descendants of Izanagi and Izanami.svg|View in SVG (for supported browsers)]]]] '''Katakurokushin''' (家宅六神) refers to the collective name of the six [[deities (Shinto)|deities]] representing (or protecting) [[households]] in [[Shinto]].
The Katakurokushin represent the materials and structure of buildings. According to the ''[[Kojiki]]'', all six deities were born after the [[Kuniumi]] (birth of the land), following the birth of '''{{ill|Ōkoto-oshi'o|ja|大事忍男神}}''', as children of {{ill|Izanagi|ja|イザナギ}} and {{ill|Izanami|ja|イザナミ}}.
== Iwatsuchi-biko-no-Kami == The first deity born is '''{{ill|Iwatsuchi-BiKo|ja|石土毘古神}}'''.
== Iwasu-hime-no-Kami == The second deity is '''{{ill|Iwasu-HiMe|ja|石巣比売神}}'''.
According to the {{ill|Koshiden|ja|古史伝}}, these two deities are alternative names for {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|ja|上筒男神|Sumiyoshi sanjin=Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Uwatatsu-no-Kami}}. In {{ill|Jinmyōkō|8=ja|9=神名考}}, it is stated that Iwatsuchi-biko-no-Kami governs over the [[earth]], while Iwasu-hime-no-Kami governs over [[:en:Sand|sand]].
== Ōtohiwake-no-Kami == The third deity is '''{{ill|Ōtohi-WaKe|ja|大戸日別神}}''', whose gender is unknown. The ''[[Kojikiden]]'' suggests that this deity may be confused with {{ill|Ōna-obi|ja|大直毘神}}. In ''Jinmyōkō'', Ōtohiwake-no-Kami is considered one of the gate deities.
== Ame-no-Fukio-no-Kami == The fourth deity is '''{{ill|Ame-no-FukiO|ja|天之吹男神}}'''. The ''Kojikiden'' interprets this deity, based on the name, as being the same as {{ill|Ibukidōnushi|ja|気吹戸主}}, who appears in the ''[[Oharae no Kotoba|Ōharae no Kotoba]]''. In ''Jinmyōkō'', this deity is considered the deity of rooftops.
== Ōya-biko-no-Kami == The fifth deity is '''{{ill|Ōya-biko|ja|大屋毘古神}}'''. This is considered a different deity from the {{ill|Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|ja|大屋毘古神|lt=Ōya-biko-no-Kami}} who appears in the myths of {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主}} and is also an alternative name for {{ill|Itonokami|ja|五十猛神}}.
== Kazamo-tsuwake-no-Oshio-no-Kami == The last deity born is '''{{ill|Kazamo-Tsuwake-no-OshiO|ja|風木津別之忍男神}}'''. Regarding the reading of the name, the original text notes that the character for "木" should be read as "mo." However, the ''Kojikiden'' suggests this annotation was mistakenly inserted later, proposing the reading "Kazaketsuwake Oshio." The ''Kojikiden'' also considers this deity to be the same as {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|ja|底筒男神|en|Sumiyoshi sanjin=Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Sokotsutsuo-no-Kami}} or {{ill|Hayasasurahime|ja|速佐須良比売}}, who appears in the ''Ōharae no Kotoba''.

The ''Kojikiden'' also considers this deity to be the same as {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|ja|底筒男神|en|Sumiyoshi sanjin=Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Sokotsutsuo-no-Kami}} or {{ill|Hayasasurahime|ja|速佐須良比売}}, who appears in the ''Ōharae no Kotoba''.

[[Image:Descendants of Izanagi and Izanami.svg|thumb|right|250px|Myth of the Birth of the Gods (Deities Born from Izanagi and Izanami) [[Media:Descendants of Izanagi and Izanami.svg|View in SVG (for supported browsers)]]]] '''Katakurokushin''' (家宅六神) refers to the collective name of the six [[deities (Shinto)|deities]] representing (or protecting) [[households]] in [[Shinto]].
The Katakurokushin represent the materials and structure of buildings. According to the ''[[Kojiki]]'', all six deities were born after the [[Kuniumi]] (birth of the land), following the birth of '''{{ill|Ōkoto-oshi'o|ja|大事忍男神}}''', as children of {{ill|Izanagi|ja|イザナギ}} and {{ill|Izanami|ja|イザナミ}}.
== Iwatsuchi-biko-no-Kami == The first deity born is '''{{ill|Iwatsuchi-BiKo|ja|石土毘古神}}'''.
== Iwasu-hime-no-Kami == The second deity is '''{{ill|Iwasu-HiMe|ja|石巣比売神}}'''.
According to the {{ill|Koshiden|ja|古史伝}}, these two deities are alternative names for {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|ja|上筒男神|Sumiyoshi sanjin=Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Uwatatsu-no-Kami}}. In {{ill|Jinmyōkō|8=ja|9=神名考}}, it is stated that Iwatsuchi-biko-no-Kami governs over the [[earth]], while Iwasu-hime-no-Kami governs over [[:en:Sand|sand]].
== Ōtohiwake-no-Kami == The third deity is '''{{ill|Ōtohi-WaKe|ja|大戸日別神}}''', whose gender is unknown. The ''[[Kojikiden]]'' suggests that this deity may be confused with {{ill|Ōna-obi|ja|大直毘神}}. In ''Jinmyōkō'', Ōtohiwake-no-Kami is considered one of the gate deities.
== Ame-no-Fukio-no-Kami == The fourth deity is '''{{ill|Ame-no-FukiO|ja|天之吹男神}}'''. The ''Kojikiden'' interprets this deity, based on the name, as being the same as {{ill|Ibukidōnushi|ja|気吹戸主}}, who appears in the ''[[Oharae no Kotoba|Ōharae no Kotoba]]''. In ''Jinmyōkō'', this deity is considered the deity of rooftops.
== Ōya-biko-no-Kami == The fifth deity is '''{{ill|Ōya-biko|ja|大屋毘古神}}'''. This is considered a different deity from the {{ill|Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|ja|大屋毘古神|lt=Ōya-biko-no-Kami}} who appears in the myths of {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主}} and is also an alternative name for {{ill|Itonokami|ja|五十猛神}}.
== Kazamo-tsuwake-no-Oshio-no-Kami == The last deity born is '''{{ill|Kazamo-Tsuwake-no-OshiO|ja|風木津別之忍男神}}'''. Regarding the reading of the name, the original text notes that the character for "木" should be read as "mo." However, the ''Kojikiden'' suggests this annotation was mistakenly inserted later, proposing the reading "Kazaketsuwake Oshio." The ''Kojikiden'' also considers this deity to be the same as {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|ja|底筒男神|en|Sumiyoshi sanjin=Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Sokotsutsuo-no-Kami}} or {{ill|Hayasasurahime|ja|速佐須良比売}}, who appears in the ''Ōharae no Kotoba''.

[[Image:Descendants of Izanagi and Izanami.svg|thumb|right|250px|Myth of the Birth of the Gods (Deities Born from Izanagi and Izanami) [[Media:Descendants of Izanagi and Izanami.svg|View in SVG (for supported browsers)]]]] '''Katakurokushin''' (家宅六神) refers to the collective name of the six [[deities (Shinto)|deities]] representing (or protecting) [[households]] in [[Shinto]].
The Katakurokushin represent the materials and structure of buildings. According to the ''[[Kojiki]]'', all six deities were born after the [[Kuniumi]] (birth of the land), following the birth of '''{{ill|Ōkoto-oshi'o|ja|大事忍男神}}''', as children of {{ill|Izanagi|ja|イザナギ}} and {{ill|Izanami|ja|イザナミ}}.
== Iwatsuchi-biko-no-Kami == The first deity born is '''{{ill|Iwatsuchi-BiKo|ja|石土毘古神}}'''.
== Iwasu-hime-no-Kami == The second deity is '''{{ill|Iwasu-HiMe|ja|石巣比売神}}'''.
According to the {{ill|Koshiden|ja|古史伝}}, these two deities are alternative names for {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|ja|上筒男神|Sumiyoshi sanjin=Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Uwatatsu-no-Kami}}. In {{ill|Jinmyōkō|8=ja|9=神名考}}, it is stated that Iwatsuchi-biko-no-Kami governs over the [[earth]], while Iwasu-hime-no-Kami governs over [[:en:Sand|sand]].
== Ōtohiwake-no-Kami == The third deity is '''{{ill|Ōtohi-WaKe|ja|大戸日別神}}''', whose gender is unknown. The ''[[Kojikiden]]'' suggests that this deity may be confused with {{ill|Ōna-obi|ja|大直毘神}}. In ''Jinmyōkō'', Ōtohiwake-no-Kami is considered one of the gate deities.
== Ame-no-Fukio-no-Kami == The fourth deity is '''{{ill|Ame-no-FukiO|ja|天之吹男神}}'''. The ''Kojikiden'' interprets this deity, based on the name, as being the same as {{ill|Ibukidōnushi|ja|気吹戸主}}, who appears in the ''[[Oharae no Kotoba|Ōharae no Kotoba]]''. In ''Jinmyōkō'', this deity is considered the deity of rooftops.
== Ōya-biko-no-Kami == The fifth deity is '''{{ill|Ōya-biko|ja|大屋毘古神}}'''. This is considered a different deity from the {{ill|Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|ja|大屋毘古神|lt=Ōya-biko-no-Kami}} who appears in the myths of {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主}} and is also an alternative name for {{ill|Itonokami|ja|五十猛神}}.
== Kazamo-tsuwake-no-Oshio-no-Kami == The last deity born is '''{{ill|Kazamo-Tsuwake-no-OshiO|ja|風木津別之忍男神}}'''. Regarding the reading of the name, the original text notes that the character for "木" should be read as "mo." However, the ''Kojikiden'' suggests this annotation was mistakenly inserted later, proposing the reading "Kazaketsuwake Oshio." The ''Kojikiden'' also considers this deity to be the same as {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|ja|底筒男神|en|Sumiyoshi sanjin=Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Sokotsutsuo-no-Kami}} or {{ill|Hayasasurahime|ja|速佐須良比売}}, who appears in the ''Ōharae no Kotoba''.

[[Image:Descendants of Izanagi and Izanami.svg|thumb|right|250px|Myth of the Birth of the Gods (Deities Born from Izanagi and Izanami) [[Media:Descendants of Izanagi and Izanami.svg|View in SVG (for supported browsers)]]]] '''Katakurokushin''' (家宅六神) refers to the collective name of the six [[deities (Shinto)|deities]] representing (or protecting) [[households]] in [[Shinto]].
The Katakurokushin represent the materials and structure of buildings. According to the ''[[Kojiki]]'', all six deities were born after the [[Kuniumi]] (birth of the land), following the birth of '''{{ill|Ōkoto-oshi'o|ja|大事忍男神}}''', as children of {{ill|Izanagi|ja|イザナギ}} and {{ill|Izanami|ja|イザナミ}}.
== Iwatsuchi-biko-no-Kami == The first deity born is '''{{ill|Iwatsuchi-BiKo|ja|石土毘古神}}'''.
== Iwasu-hime-no-Kami == The second deity is '''{{ill|Iwasu-HiMe|ja|石巣比売神}}'''.
According to the {{ill|Koshiden|ja|古史伝}}, these two deities are alternative names for {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|ja|上筒男神|Sumiyoshi sanjin=Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Uwatatsu-no-Kami}}. In {{ill|Jinmyōkō|8=ja|9=神名考}}, it is stated that Iwatsuchi-biko-no-Kami governs over the [[earth]], while Iwasu-hime-no-Kami governs over [[:en:Sand|sand]].
== Ōtohiwake-no-Kami == The third deity is '''{{ill|Ōtohi-WaKe|ja|大戸日別神}}''', whose gender is unknown. The ''[[Kojikiden]]'' suggests that this deity may be confused with {{ill|Ōna-obi|ja|大直毘神}}. In ''Jinmyōkō'', Ōtohiwake-no-Kami is considered one of the gate deities.
== Ame-no-Fukio-no-Kami == The fourth deity is '''{{ill|Ame-no-FukiO|ja|天之吹男神}}'''. The ''Kojikiden'' interprets this deity, based on the name, as being the same as {{ill|Ibukidōnushi|ja|気吹戸主}}, who appears in the ''[[Oharae no Kotoba|Ōharae no Kotoba]]''. In ''Jinmyōkō'', this deity is considered the deity of rooftops.
== Ōya-biko-no-Kami == The fifth deity is '''{{ill|Ōya-biko|ja|大屋毘古神}}'''. This is considered a different deity from the {{ill|Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|ja|大屋毘古神|lt=Ōya-biko-no-Kami}} who appears in the myths of {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主}} and is also an alternative name for {{ill|Itonokami|ja|五十猛神}}.
== Kazamo-tsuwake-no-Oshio-no-Kami == The last deity born is '''{{ill|Kazamo-Tsuwake-no-OshiO|ja|風木津別之忍男神}}'''. Regarding the reading of the name, the original text notes that the character for "木" should be read as "mo." However, the ''Kojikiden'' suggests this annotation was mistakenly inserted later, proposing the reading "Kazaketsuwake Oshio." The ''Kojikiden'' also considers this deity to be the same as {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|ja|底筒男神|en|Sumiyoshi sanjin=Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Sokotsutsuo-no-Kami}} or {{ill|Hayasasurahime|ja|速佐須良比売}}, who appears in the ''Ōharae no Kotoba''.

[[Image:Descendants of Izanagi and Izanami.svg|thumb|right|250px|Myth of the Birth of the Gods (Deities Born from Izanagi and Izanami) [[Media:Descendants of Izanagi and Izanami.svg|View in SVG (for supported browsers)]]]] '''Katakurokushin''' (家宅六神) refers to the collective name of the six [[deities (Shinto)|deities]] representing (or protecting) [[households]] in [[Shinto]].
The Katakurokushin represent the materials and structure of buildings. According to the ''[[Kojiki]]'', all six deities were born after the [[Kuniumi]] (birth of the land), following the birth of '''{{ill|Ōkoto-oshi'o|ja|大事忍男神}}''', as children of {{ill|Izanagi|ja|イザナギ}} and {{ill|Izanami|ja|イザナミ}}.
== Iwatsuchi-biko-no-Kami == The first deity born is '''{{ill|Iwatsuchi-BiKo|ja|石土毘古神}}'''.
== Iwasu-hime-no-Kami == The second deity is '''{{ill|Iwasu-HiMe|ja|石巣比売神}}'''.
According to the {{ill|Koshiden|ja|古史伝}}, these two deities are alternative names for {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|ja|上筒男神|Sumiyoshi sanjin=Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Uwatatsu-no-Kami}}. In {{ill|Jinmyōkō|8=ja|9=神名考}}, it is stated that Iwatsuchi-biko-no-Kami governs over the [[earth]], while Iwasu-hime-no-Kami governs over [[:en:Sand|sand]].
== Ōtohiwake-no-Kami == The third deity is '''{{ill|Ōtohi-WaKe|ja|大戸日別神}}''', whose gender is unknown. The ''[[Kojikiden]]'' suggests that this deity may be confused with {{ill|Ōna-obi|ja|大直毘神}}. In ''Jinmyōkō'', Ōtohiwake-no-Kami is considered one of the gate deities.
== Ame-no-Fukio-no-Kami == The fourth deity is '''{{ill|Ame-no-FukiO|ja|天之吹男神}}'''. The ''Kojikiden'' interprets this deity, based on the name, as being the same as {{ill|Ibukidōnushi|ja|気吹戸主}}, who appears in the ''[[Oharae no Kotoba|Ōharae no Kotoba]]''. In ''Jinmyōkō'', this deity is considered the deity of rooftops.
== Ōya-biko-no-Kami == The fifth deity is '''{{ill|Ōya-biko|ja|大屋毘古神}}'''. This is considered a different deity from the {{ill|Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|ja|大屋毘古神|lt=Ōya-biko-no-Kami}} who appears in the myths of {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主}} and is also an alternative name for {{ill|Itonokami|ja|五十猛神}}.
== Kazamo-tsuwake-no-Oshio-no-Kami == The last deity born is '''{{ill|Kazamo-Tsuwake-no-OshiO|ja|風木津別之忍男神}}'''. Regarding the reading of the name, the original text notes that the character for "木" should be read as "mo." However, the ''Kojikiden'' suggests this annotation was mistakenly inserted later, proposing the reading "Kazaketsuwake Oshio." The ''Kojikiden'' also considers this deity to be the same as {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|ja|底筒男神|en|Sumiyoshi sanjin=Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Sokotsutsuo-no-Kami}} or {{ill|Hayasasurahime|ja|速佐須良比売}}, who appears in the ''Ōharae no Kotoba''.

revision.text:
[[Image:Descendants of Izanagi and Izanami.svg|thumb|right|250px|Myth of the Birth of the Gods (Deities Born from Izanagi and Izanami) [[Media:Descendants of Izanagi and Izanami.svg|View in SVG (for supported browsers)]]]] '''Katakurokushin''' (家宅六神) refers to the collective name of the six [[deities (Shinto)|deities]] representing (or protecting) [[households]] in [[Shinto]].
The Katakurokushin represent the materials and structure of buildings. According to the ''[[Kojiki]]'', all six deities were born after the [[Kuniumi]] (birth of the land), following the birth of '''{{ill|Ōkoto-oshi'o|ja|大事忍男神}}''', as children of {{ill|Izanagi|ja|イザナギ}} and {{ill|Izanami|ja|イザナミ}}.
== Iwatsuchi-biko-no-Kami == The first deity born is '''{{ill|Iwatsuchi-BiKo|ja|石土毘古神}}'''.
== Iwasu-hime-no-Kami == The second deity is '''{{ill|Iwasu-HiMe|ja|石巣比売神}}'''.
According to the {{ill|Koshiden|ja|古史伝}}, these two deities are alternative names for {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|ja|上筒男神|Sumiyoshi sanjin=Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Uwatatsu-no-Kami}}. In {{ill|Jinmyōkō|8=ja|9=神名考}}, it is stated that Iwatsuchi-biko-no-Kami governs over the [[earth]], while Iwasu-hime-no-Kami governs over [[:en:Sand|sand]].
== Ōtohiwake-no-Kami == The third deity is '''{{ill|Ōtohi-WaKe|ja|大戸日別神}}''', whose gender is unknown. The ''[[Kojikiden]]'' suggests that this deity may be confused with {{ill|Ōna-obi|ja|大直毘神}}. In ''Jinmyōkō'', Ōtohiwake-no-Kami is considered one of the gate deities.
== Ame-no-Fukio-no-Kami == The fourth deity is '''{{ill|Ame-no-FukiO|ja|天之吹男神}}'''. The ''Kojikiden'' interprets this deity, based on the name, as being the same as {{ill|Ibukidōnushi|ja|気吹戸主}}, who appears in the ''[[Oharae no Kotoba|Ōharae no Kotoba]]''. In ''Jinmyōkō'', this deity is considered the deity of rooftops.
== Ōya-biko-no-Kami == The fifth deity is '''{{ill|Ōya-biko|ja|大屋毘古神}}'''. This is considered a different deity from the {{ill|Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|ja|大屋毘古神|lt=Ōya-biko-no-Kami}} who appears in the myths of {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主}} and is also an alternative name for {{ill|Itonokami|ja|五十猛神}}.
== Kazamo-tsuwake-no-Oshio-no-Kami == The last deity born is '''{{ill|Kazamo-Tsuwake-no-OshiO|ja|風木津別之忍男神}}'''. Regarding the reading of the name, the original text notes that the character for "木" should be read as "mo." However, the ''Kojikiden'' suggests this annotation was mistakenly inserted later, proposing the reading "Kazaketsuwake Oshio." The ''Kojikiden'' also considers this deity to be the same as {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|ja|底筒男神|en|Sumiyoshi sanjin=Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Sokotsutsuo-no-Kami}} or {{ill|Hayasasurahime|ja|速佐須良比売}}, who appears in the ''Ōharae no Kotoba''.== See Also == * [[List of Japanese deities]] * [[Kamiumi]]

revision.comment: Text replacement - "== See Also ==" to " == See Also! =="
revision.text:
[[Image:Descendants of Izanagi and Izanami.svg|thumb|right|250px|Myth of the Birth of the Gods (Deities Born from Izanagi and Izanami) [[Media:Descendants of Izanagi and Izanami.svg|View in SVG (for supported browsers)]]]] '''Katakurokushin''' (家宅六神) refers to the collective name of the six [[deities (Shinto)|deities]] representing (or protecting) [[households]] in [[Shinto]].
The Katakurokushin represent the materials and structure of buildings. According to the ''[[Kojiki]]'', all six deities were born after the [[Kuniumi]] (birth of the land), following the birth of '''{{ill|Ōkoto-oshi'o|ja|大事忍男神}}''', as children of {{ill|Izanagi|ja|イザナギ}} and {{ill|Izanami|ja|イザナミ}}.
== Iwatsuchi-biko-no-Kami == The first deity born is '''{{ill|Iwatsuchi-BiKo|ja|石土毘古神}}'''.
== Iwasu-hime-no-Kami == The second deity is '''{{ill|Iwasu-HiMe|ja|石巣比売神}}'''.
According to the {{ill|Koshiden|ja|古史伝}}, these two deities are alternative names for {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|ja|上筒男神|Sumiyoshi sanjin=Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Uwatatsu-no-Kami}}. In {{ill|Jinmyōkō|8=ja|9=神名考}}, it is stated that Iwatsuchi-biko-no-Kami governs over the [[earth]], while Iwasu-hime-no-Kami governs over [[:en:Sand|sand]].
== Ōtohiwake-no-Kami == The third deity is '''{{ill|Ōtohi-WaKe|ja|大戸日別神}}''', whose gender is unknown. The ''[[Kojikiden]]'' suggests that this deity may be confused with {{ill|Ōna-obi|ja|大直毘神}}. In ''Jinmyōkō'', Ōtohiwake-no-Kami is considered one of the gate deities.
== Ame-no-Fukio-no-Kami == The fourth deity is '''{{ill|Ame-no-FukiO|ja|天之吹男神}}'''. The ''Kojikiden'' interprets this deity, based on the name, as being the same as {{ill|Ibukidōnushi|ja|気吹戸主}}, who appears in the ''[[Oharae no Kotoba|Ōharae no Kotoba]]''. In ''Jinmyōkō'', this deity is considered the deity of rooftops.
== Ōya-biko-no-Kami == The fifth deity is '''{{ill|Ōya-biko|ja|大屋毘古神}}'''. This is considered a different deity from the {{ill|Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|ja|大屋毘古神|lt=Ōya-biko-no-Kami}} who appears in the myths of {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主}} and is also an alternative name for {{ill|Itonokami|ja|五十猛神}}.
== Kazamo-tsuwake-no-Oshio-no-Kami == The last deity born is '''{{ill|Kazamo-Tsuwake-no-OshiO|ja|風木津別之忍男神}}'''. Regarding the reading of the name, the original text notes that the character for "木" should be read as "mo." However, the ''Kojikiden'' suggests this annotation was mistakenly inserted later, proposing the reading "Kazaketsuwake Oshio." The ''Kojikiden'' also considers this deity to be the same as {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|ja|底筒男神|en|Sumiyoshi sanjin=Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Sokotsutsuo-no-Kami}} or {{ill|Hayasasurahime|ja|速佐須良比売}}, who appears in the ''Ōharae no Kotoba''. == See Also! == * [[List of Japanese deities]] * [[Kamiumi]]

revision.comment: Moving from [[Category:Original pages]] to [[Category:completion tier 4]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
[[Image:Descendants of Izanagi and Izanami.svg|thumb|right|250px|Myth of the Birth of the Gods (Deities Born from Izanagi and Izanami) [[Media:Descendants of Izanagi and Izanami.svg|View in SVG (for supported browsers)]]]] '''Katakurokushin''' (家宅六神) refers to the collective name of the six [[deities (Shinto)|deities]] representing (or protecting) [[households]] in [[Shinto]].
The Katakurokushin represent the materials and structure of buildings. According to the ''[[Kojiki]]'', all six deities were born after the [[Kuniumi]] (birth of the land), following the birth of '''{{ill|Ōkoto-oshi'o|ja|大事忍男神}}''', as children of {{ill|Izanagi|ja|イザナギ}} and {{ill|Izanami|ja|イザナミ}}.
== Iwatsuchi-biko-no-Kami == The first deity born is '''{{ill|Iwatsuchi-BiKo|ja|石土毘古神}}'''.
== Iwasu-hime-no-Kami == The second deity is '''{{ill|Iwasu-HiMe|ja|石巣比売神}}'''.
According to the {{ill|Koshiden|ja|古史伝}}, these two deities are alternative names for {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|ja|上筒男神|Sumiyoshi sanjin=Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Uwatatsu-no-Kami}}. In {{ill|Jinmyōkō|8=ja|9=神名考}}, it is stated that Iwatsuchi-biko-no-Kami governs over the [[earth]], while Iwasu-hime-no-Kami governs over [[:en:Sand|sand]].
== Ōtohiwake-no-Kami == The third deity is '''{{ill|Ōtohi-WaKe|ja|大戸日別神}}''', whose gender is unknown. The ''[[Kojikiden]]'' suggests that this deity may be confused with {{ill|Ōna-obi|ja|大直毘神}}. In ''Jinmyōkō'', Ōtohiwake-no-Kami is considered one of the gate deities.
== Ame-no-Fukio-no-Kami == The fourth deity is '''{{ill|Ame-no-FukiO|ja|天之吹男神}}'''. The ''Kojikiden'' interprets this deity, based on the name, as being the same as {{ill|Ibukidōnushi|ja|気吹戸主}}, who appears in the ''[[Oharae no Kotoba|Ōharae no Kotoba]]''. In ''Jinmyōkō'', this deity is considered the deity of rooftops.
== Ōya-biko-no-Kami == The fifth deity is '''{{ill|Ōya-biko|ja|大屋毘古神}}'''. This is considered a different deity from the {{ill|Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|ja|大屋毘古神|lt=Ōya-biko-no-Kami}} who appears in the myths of {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主}} and is also an alternative name for {{ill|Itonokami|ja|五十猛神}}.
== Kazamo-tsuwake-no-Oshio-no-Kami == The last deity born is '''{{ill|Kazamo-Tsuwake-no-OshiO|ja|風木津別之忍男神}}'''. Regarding the reading of the name, the original text notes that the character for "木" should be read as "mo." However, the ''Kojikiden'' suggests this annotation was mistakenly inserted later, proposing the reading "Kazaketsuwake Oshio." The ''Kojikiden'' also considers this deity to be the same as {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|ja|底筒男神|en|Sumiyoshi sanjin=Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Sokotsutsuo-no-Kami}} or {{ill|Hayasasurahime|ja|速佐須良比売}}, who appears in the ''Ōharae no Kotoba''. == See Also == * [[List of Japanese deities]] * [[Kamiumi]]

[[Image:Descendants of Izanagi and Izanami.svg|thumb|right|250px|Myth of the Birth of the Gods (Deities Born from Izanagi and Izanami) [[Media:Descendants of Izanagi and Izanami.svg|View in SVG (for supported browsers)]]]] '''Katakurokushin''' (家宅六神) refers to the collective name of the six [[deities (Shinto)|deities]] representing (or protecting) [[households]] in [[Shinto]].
The Katakurokushin represent the materials and structure of buildings. According to the ''[[Kojiki]]'', all six deities were born after the [[Kuniumi]] (birth of the land), following the birth of '''{{ill|Ōkoto-oshi'o|ja|大事忍男神}}''', as children of {{ill|Izanagi|ja|イザナギ}} and {{ill|Izanami|ja|イザナミ}}.
== Iwatsuchi-biko-no-Kami == The first deity born is '''{{ill|Iwatsuchi-BiKo|ja|石土毘古神}}'''.
== Iwasu-hime-no-Kami == The second deity is '''{{ill|Iwasu-HiMe|ja|石巣比売神}}'''.
According to the {{ill|Koshiden|ja|古史伝}}, these two deities are alternative names for {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|ja|上筒男神|Sumiyoshi sanjin=Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Uwatatsu-no-Kami}}. In {{ill|Jinmyōkō|8=ja|9=神名考}}, it is stated that Iwatsuchi-biko-no-Kami governs over the [[earth]], while Iwasu-hime-no-Kami governs over [[:en:Sand|sand]].
== Ōtohiwake-no-Kami == The third deity is '''{{ill|Ōtohi-WaKe|ja|大戸日別神}}''', whose gender is unknown. The ''[[Kojikiden]]'' suggests that this deity may be confused with {{ill|Ōna-obi|ja|大直毘神}}. In ''Jinmyōkō'', Ōtohiwake-no-Kami is considered one of the gate deities.
== Ame-no-Fukio-no-Kami == The fourth deity is '''{{ill|Ame-no-FukiO|ja|天之吹男神}}'''. The ''Kojikiden'' interprets this deity, based on the name, as being the same as {{ill|Ibukidōnushi|ja|気吹戸主}}, who appears in the ''[[Oharae no Kotoba|Ōharae no Kotoba]]''. In ''Jinmyōkō'', this deity is considered the deity of rooftops.
== Oyabiko-no-Kami == The fifth deity is '''{{ill|Oyabiko|ja|大屋毘古神}}'''. This is considered a different deity from the {{ill|Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|ja|大屋毘古神|lt=Oyabiko-no-Kami}} who appears in the myths of {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主}} and is also an alternative name for {{ill|Itonokami|ja|五十猛神}}.
== Kazamo-tsuwake-no-Oshio-no-Kami == The last deity born is '''{{ill|Kazamo-Tsuwake-no-OshiO|ja|風木津別之忍男神}}'''. Regarding the reading of the name, the original text notes that the character for "木" should be read as "mo." However, the ''Kojikiden'' suggests this annotation was mistakenly inserted later, proposing the reading "Kazaketsuwake Oshio." The ''Kojikiden'' also considers this deity to be the same as {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|ja|底筒男神|en|Sumiyoshi sanjin=Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Sokotsutsuo-no-Kami}} or {{ill|Hayasasurahime|ja|速佐須良比売}}, who appears in the ''Ōharae no Kotoba''. == See Also == * [[List of Japanese deities]] * [[Kamiumi]]

[[Image:Descendants of Izanagi and Izanami.svg|thumb|right|250px|Myth of the Birth of the Gods (Deities Born from Izanagi and Izanami) [[Media:Descendants of Izanagi and Izanami.svg|View in SVG (for supported browsers)]]]] '''Katakurokushin''' (家宅六神) refers to the collective name of the six [[deities (Shinto)|deities]] representing (or protecting) [[households]] in [[Shinto]].
The Katakurokushin represent the materials and structure of buildings. According to the ''[[Kojiki]]'', all six deities were born after the [[Kuniumi]] (birth of the land), following the birth of '''{{ill|Ōkoto-oshi'o|ja|大事忍男神}}''', as children of {{ill|Izanagi|ja|イザナギ}} and {{ill|Izanami|ja|イザナミ}}.
== Iwatsuchi-biko-no-Kami == The first deity born is '''{{ill|Iwatsuchi-BiKo|ja|石土毘古神}}'''.
== Iwasu-hime-no-Kami == The second deity is '''{{ill|Iwasu-HiMe|ja|石巣比売神}}'''.
According to the {{ill|Koshiden|ja|古史伝}}, these two deities are alternative names for {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|ja|上筒男神|Sumiyoshi sanjin=Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Uwatatsu-no-Kami}}. In {{ill|Jinmyōkō|8=ja|9=神名考}}, it is stated that Iwatsuchi-biko-no-Kami governs over the [[earth]], while Iwasu-hime-no-Kami governs over [[:en:Sand|sand]].
== Ōtohiwake-no-Kami == The third deity is '''{{ill|Ōtohi-WaKe|ja|大戸日別神}}''', whose gender is unknown. The ''[[Kojikiden]]'' suggests that this deity may be confused with {{ill|Ōna-obi|ja|大直毘神}}. In ''Jinmyōkō'', Ōtohiwake-no-Kami is considered one of the gate deities.
== Ame-no-Fukio-no-Kami == The fourth deity is '''{{ill|Ame-no-FukiO|ja|天之吹男神}}'''. The ''Kojikiden'' interprets this deity, based on the name, as being the same as {{ill|Ibukidōnushi|ja|気吹戸主}}, who appears in the ''[[Oharae no Kotoba|Ōharae no Kotoba]]''. In ''Jinmyōkō'', this deity is considered the deity of rooftops.
== Oyabiko-no-Kami == The fifth deity is '''{{ill|Oyabiko|ja|大屋毘古神}}'''. This is considered a different deity from the {{ill|Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|ja|大屋毘古神|lt=Oyabiko-no-Kami}} who appears in the myths of {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主}} and is also an alternative name for {{ill|Itonokami|ja|五十猛神}}.
== Kazamo-tsuwake-no-Oshio-no-Kami == The last deity born is '''{{ill|Kazamo-Tsuwake-no-OshiO|ja|風木津別之忍男神}}'''. Regarding the reading of the name, the original text notes that the character for "木" should be read as "mo." However, the ''Kojikiden'' suggests this annotation was mistakenly inserted later, proposing the reading "Kazaketsuwake Oshio." The ''Kojikiden'' also considers this deity to be the same as {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|ja|底筒男神|en|Sumiyoshi sanjin=Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Sokotsutsuo-no-Kami}} or {{ill|Hayasasurahime|ja|速佐須良比売}}, who appears in the ''Ōharae no Kotoba''. == See Also == * [[List of Japanese deities]] * [[Kamiumi]]

[[Image:Descendants of Izanagi and Izanami.svg|thumb|right|250px|Myth of the Birth of the Gods (Deities Born from Izanagi and Izanami) [[Media:Descendants of Izanagi and Izanami.svg|View in SVG (for supported browsers)]]]] '''Katakurokushin''' (家宅六神) refers to the collective name of the six [[deities (Shinto)|deities]] representing (or protecting) [[households]] in [[Shinto]].
The Katakurokushin represent the materials and structure of buildings. According to the ''[[Kojiki]]'', all six deities were born after the [[Kuniumi]] (birth of the land), following the birth of '''{{ill|Ōkoto-oshi'o|ja|大事忍男神}}''', as children of {{ill|Izanagi|ja|イザナギ}} and {{ill|Izanami|ja|イザナミ}}.
== Iwatsuchi-biko-no-Kami == The first deity born is '''{{ill|Iwatsuchi-BiKo|ja|石土毘古神}}'''.
== Iwasu-hime-no-Kami == The second deity is '''{{ill|Iwasu-HiMe|ja|石巣比売神}}'''.
According to the {{ill|Koshiden|ja|古史伝}}, these two deities are alternative names for {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|ja|上筒男神|Sumiyoshi sanjin=Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Uwatatsu-no-Kami}}. In {{ill|Jinmyōkō|8=ja|9=神名考}}, it is stated that Iwatsuchi-biko-no-Kami governs over the [[earth]], while Iwasu-hime-no-Kami governs over [[:en:Sand|sand]].
== Ōtohiwake-no-Kami == The third deity is '''{{ill|Ōtohi-WaKe|ja|大戸日別神}}''', whose gender is unknown. The ''[[Kojikiden]]'' suggests that this deity may be confused with {{ill|Ōna-obi|ja|大直毘神}}. In ''Jinmyōkō'', Ōtohiwake-no-Kami is considered one of the gate deities.
== Ame-no-Fukio-no-Kami == The fourth deity is '''{{ill|Ame-no-FukiO|ja|天之吹男神}}'''. The ''Kojikiden'' interprets this deity, based on the name, as being the same as {{ill|Ibukidōnushi|ja|気吹戸主}}, who appears in the ''[[Oharae no Kotoba|Ōharae no Kotoba]]''. In ''Jinmyōkō'', this deity is considered the deity of rooftops.
== Oyabiko-no-Kami == The fifth deity is '''{{ill|Oyabiko|ja|大屋毘古神}}'''. This is considered a different deity from the {{ill|Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|ja|大屋毘古神|lt=Oyabiko-no-Kami}} who appears in the myths of {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主}} and is also an alternative name for {{ill|Itonokami|ja|五十猛神}}.
== Kazamo-tsuwake-no-Oshio-no-Kami == The last deity born is '''{{ill|Kazamo-Tsuwake-no-OshiO|ja|風木津別之忍男神}}'''. Regarding the reading of the name, the original text notes that the character for "木" should be read as "mo." However, the ''Kojikiden'' suggests this annotation was mistakenly inserted later, proposing the reading "Kazaketsuwake Oshio." The ''Kojikiden'' also considers this deity to be the same as {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|ja|底筒男神|en|Sumiyoshi sanjin=Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Sokotsutsuo-no-Kami}} or {{ill|Hayasasurahime|ja|速佐須良比売}}, who appears in the ''Ōharae no Kotoba''. == See Also == * [[List of Japanese deities]] * [[Kamiumi]]

[[Image:Descendants of Izanagi and Izanami.svg|thumb|right|250px|Myth of the Birth of the Gods (Deities Born from Izanagi and Izanami) [[Media:Descendants of Izanagi and Izanami.svg|View in SVG (for supported browsers)]]]] '''Katakurokushin''' (家宅六神) refers to the collective name of the six [[deities (Shinto)|deities]] representing (or protecting) [[households]] in [[Shinto]].
The Katakurokushin represent the materials and structure of buildings. According to the ''[[Kojiki]]'', all six deities were born after the [[Kuniumi]] (birth of the land), following the birth of '''{{ill|Ōkoto-oshi'o|ja|大事忍男神}}''', as children of {{ill|Izanagi|ja|イザナギ}} and {{ill|Izanami|ja|イザナミ}}.
== Iwatsuchi-biko-no-Kami == The first deity born is '''{{ill|Iwatsuchi-BiKo|ja|石土毘古神}}'''.
== Iwasu-hime-no-Kami == The second deity is '''{{ill|Iwasu-HiMe|ja|石巣比売神}}'''.
According to the {{ill|Koshiden|ja|古史伝}}, these two deities are alternative names for {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|ja|上筒男神|Sumiyoshi sanjin=Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Uwatatsu-no-Kami}}. In {{ill|Jinmyōkō|8=ja|9=神名考}}, it is stated that Iwatsuchi-biko-no-Kami governs over the [[earth]], while Iwasu-hime-no-Kami governs over [[:en:Sand|sand]].
== Ōtohiwake-no-Kami == The third deity is '''{{ill|Ōtohi-WaKe|ja|大戸日別神}}''', whose gender is unknown. The ''[[Kojikiden]]'' suggests that this deity may be confused with {{ill|Ōna-obi|ja|大直毘神}}. In ''Jinmyōkō'', Ōtohiwake-no-Kami is considered one of the gate deities.
== Ame-no-Fukio-no-Kami == The fourth deity is '''{{ill|Ame-no-FukiO|ja|天之吹男神}}'''. The ''Kojikiden'' interprets this deity, based on the name, as being the same as {{ill|Ibukidōnushi|ja|気吹戸主}}, who appears in the ''[[Oharae no Kotoba|Ōharae no Kotoba]]''. In ''Jinmyōkō'', this deity is considered the deity of rooftops.
== Oyabiko-no-Kami == The fifth deity is '''{{ill|Oyabiko|ja|大屋毘古神}}'''. This is considered a different deity from the {{ill|Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|ja|大屋毘古神|lt=Oyabiko-no-Kami}} who appears in the myths of {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主}} and is also an alternative name for {{ill|Itonokami|ja|五十猛神}}.
== Kazamo-tsuwake-no-Oshio-no-Kami == The last deity born is '''{{ill|Kazamo-Tsuwake-no-OshiO|ja|風木津別之忍男神}}'''. Regarding the reading of the name, the original text notes that the character for "木" should be read as "mo." However, the ''Kojikiden'' suggests this annotation was mistakenly inserted later, proposing the reading "Kazaketsuwake Oshio." The ''Kojikiden'' also considers this deity to be the same as {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|ja|底筒男神|en|Sumiyoshi sanjin=Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Sokotsutsuo-no-Kami}} or {{ill|Hayasasurahime|ja|速佐須良比売}}, who appears in the ''Ōharae no Kotoba''. == See Also == * [[List of Japanese deities]] * [[Kamiumi]]

According to the {{ill|Koshiden|ja|古史伝|12=draft}}, these two deities are alternative names for {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|ja|上筒男神|Sumiyoshi sanjin=Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Uwatatsu-no-Kami|12=draft}}. In {{ill|Jinmyōkō|8=ja|9=神名考|12=draft}}, it is stated that Iwatsuchi-biko-no-Kami governs over the [[earth]], while Iwasu-hime-no-Kami governs over [[:en:Sand|sand]].
== Ōtohiwake-no-Kami == The third deity is '''{{ill|Ōtohi-WaKe|ja|大戸日別神|12=draft}}''', whose gender is unknown. The ''[[Kojikiden]]'' suggests that this deity may be confused with {{ill|Ōna-obi|ja|大直毘神|12=draft}}. In ''Jinmyōkō'', Ōtohiwake-no-Kami is considered one of the gate deities.
== Ame-no-Fukio-no-Kami == The fourth deity is '''{{ill|Ame-no-FukiO|ja|天之吹男神|12=draft}}'''. The ''Kojikiden'' interprets this deity, based on the name, as being the same as {{ill|Ibukidōnushi|ja|気吹戸主|12=draft}}, who appears in the ''[[Oharae no Kotoba|Ōharae no Kotoba]]''. In ''Jinmyōkō'', this deity is considered the deity of rooftops.
== Oyabiko-no-Kami == The fifth deity is '''{{ill|Oyabiko|ja|大屋毘古神|12=draft}}'''. This is considered a different deity from the {{ill|Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|ja|大屋毘古神|lt=Oyabiko-no-Kami|12=draft}} who appears in the myths of {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主|12=draft}} and is also an alternative name for {{ill|Itonokami|ja|五十猛神|12=draft}}.
== Kazamo-tsuwake-no-Oshio-no-Kami == The last deity born is '''{{ill|Kazamo-Tsuwake-no-OshiO|ja|風木津別之忍男神|12=draft}}'''. Regarding the reading of the name, the original text notes that the character for "木" should be read as "mo." However, the ''Kojikiden'' suggests this annotation was mistakenly inserted later, proposing the reading "Kazaketsuwake Oshio." The ''Kojikiden'' also considers this deity to be the same as {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|ja|底筒男神|en|Sumiyoshi sanjin=Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Sokotsutsuo-no-Kami|12=draft}} or {{ill|Hayasasurahime|ja|速佐須良比売|12=draft}}, who appears in the ''Ōharae no Kotoba''. == See Also == * [[List of Japanese deities]] * [[Kamiumi]]
{{Japanese mythology}} {{Shinto-stub}}

revision.text:
[[Image:Descendants of Izanagi and Izanami.svg|thumb|right|250px|Myth of the Birth of the Gods (Deities Born from Izanagi and Izanami) [[Media:Descendants of Izanagi and Izanami.svg|View in SVG (for supported browsers)]]]] '''Katakurokushin''' (家宅六神) refers to the collective name of the six [[deities (Shinto)|deities]] representing (or protecting) [[households]] in [[Shinto]].
The Katakurokushin represent the materials and structure of buildings. According to the ''[[Kojiki]]'', all six deities were born after the [[Kuniumi]] (birth of the land), following the birth of '''{{ill|Ōkoto-oshi'o|ja|大事忍男神|12=draft}}''', as children of {{ill|Izanagi|ja|イザナギ|12=draft}} and {{ill|Izanami|ja|イザナミ|12=draft}}.
== Iwatsuchi-biko-no-Kami == The first deity born is '''{{ill|Iwatsuchi-BiKo|ja|石土毘古神|12=draft}}'''.
== Iwasu-hime-no-Kami == The second deity is '''{{ill|Iwasu-HiMe|ja|石巣比売神|12=draft}}'''.
According to the {{ill|Koshiden|ja|古史伝|12=draft}}, these two deities are alternative names for {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|ja|上筒男神|Sumiyoshi sanjin=Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Uwatatsu-no-Kami|12=draft}}. In {{ill|Jinmyōkō|8=ja|9=神名考|12=draft}}, it is stated that Iwatsuchi-biko-no-Kami governs over the [[earth]], while Iwasu-hime-no-Kami governs over [[:en:Sand|sand]].
== Ōtohiwake-no-Kami == The third deity is '''{{ill|Ōtohi-WaKe|ja|大戸日別神|12=draft}}''', whose gender is unknown. The ''[[Kojikiden]]'' suggests that this deity may be confused with {{ill|Ōna-obi|ja|大直毘神|12=draft}}. In ''Jinmyōkō'', Ōtohiwake-no-Kami is considered one of the gate deities.
== Ame-no-Fukio-no-Kami == The fourth deity is '''{{ill|Ame-no-FukiO|ja|天之吹男神|12=draft}}'''. The ''Kojikiden'' interprets this deity, based on the name, as being the same as {{ill|Ibukidōnushi|ja|気吹戸主|12=draft}}, who appears in the ''[[Oharae no Kotoba|Ōharae no Kotoba]]''. In ''Jinmyōkō'', this deity is considered the deity of rooftops.
== Oyabiko-no-Kami == The fifth deity is '''{{ill|Oyabiko|ja|大屋毘古神|12=draft}}'''. This is considered a different deity from the {{ill|Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|ja|大屋毘古神|lt=Oyabiko-no-Kami|12=draft}} who appears in the myths of {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主|12=draft}} and is also an alternative name for {{ill|Itonokami|ja|五十猛神|12=draft}}.
== Kazamo-tsuwake-no-Oshio-no-Kami == The last deity born is '''{{ill|Kazamo-Tsuwake-no-OshiO|ja|風木津別之忍男神|12=draft}}'''. Regarding the reading of the name, the original text notes that the character for "木" should be read as "mo." However, the ''Kojikiden'' suggests this annotation was mistakenly inserted later, proposing the reading "Kazaketsuwake Oshio." The ''Kojikiden'' also considers this deity to be the same as {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|ja|底筒男神|en|Sumiyoshi sanjin=Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Sokotsutsuo-no-Kami|12=draft}} or {{ill|Hayasasurahime|ja|速佐須良比売|12=draft}}, who appears in the ''Ōharae no Kotoba''.
== See Also == * [[List of Japanese deities]] * [[Kamiumi]]

revision.text:
[[Image:Descendants of Izanagi and Izanami.svg|thumb|right|250px|Myth of the Birth of the Gods (Deities Born from Izanagi and Izanami) [[Media:Descendants of Izanagi and Izanami.svg|View in SVG (for supported browsers)]]]] '''Katakurokushin''' (家宅六神) refers to the collective name of the six [[deities (Shinto)|deities]] representing (or protecting) [[households]] in [[Shinto]].
The Katakurokushin represent the materials and structure of buildings. According to the ''[[Kojiki]]'', all six deities were born after the [[Kuniumi]] (birth of the land), following the birth of '''{{ill|Ōkoto-oshi'o|ja|大事忍男神|12=draft|12=draft}}''', as children of {{ill|Izanagi|ja|イザナギ|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Izanami|ja|イザナミ|12=draft|12=draft}}.
== Iwatsuchi-biko-no-Kami == The first deity born is '''{{ill|Iwatsuchi-BiKo|ja|石土毘古神|12=draft|12=draft}}'''.
== Iwasu-hime-no-Kami == The second deity is '''{{ill|Iwasu-HiMe|ja|石巣比売神|12=draft|12=draft}}'''.
According to the {{ill|Koshiden|ja|古史伝|12=draft|12=draft}}, these two deities are alternative names for {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|ja|上筒男神|Sumiyoshi sanjin=Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Uwatatsu-no-Kami|12=draft|12=draft}}. In {{ill|Jinmyōkō|8=ja|9=神名考|12=draft|12=draft}}, it is stated that Iwatsuchi-biko-no-Kami governs over the [[earth]], while Iwasu-hime-no-Kami governs over [[:en:Sand|sand]].
== Ōtohiwake-no-Kami == The third deity is '''{{ill|Ōtohi-WaKe|ja|大戸日別神|12=draft|12=draft}}''', whose gender is unknown. The ''[[Kojikiden]]'' suggests that this deity may be confused with {{ill|Ōna-obi|ja|大直毘神|12=draft|12=draft}}. In ''Jinmyōkō'', Ōtohiwake-no-Kami is considered one of the gate deities.
== Ame-no-Fukio-no-Kami == The fourth deity is '''{{ill|Ame-no-FukiO|ja|天之吹男神|12=draft|12=draft}}'''. The ''Kojikiden'' interprets this deity, based on the name, as being the same as {{ill|Ibukidōnushi|ja|気吹戸主|12=draft|12=draft}}, who appears in the ''[[Oharae no Kotoba|Ōharae no Kotoba]]''. In ''Jinmyōkō'', this deity is considered the deity of rooftops.
== Oyabiko-no-Kami == The fifth deity is '''{{ill|Oyabiko|ja|大屋毘古神|12=draft|12=draft}}'''. This is considered a different deity from the {{ill|Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|ja|大屋毘古神|lt=Oyabiko-no-Kami|12=draft|12=draft}} who appears in the myths of {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主|12=draft|12=draft}} and is also an alternative name for {{ill|Itonokami|ja|五十猛神|12=draft|12=draft}}.
== Kazamo-tsuwake-no-Oshio-no-Kami == The last deity born is '''{{ill|Kazamo-Tsuwake-no-OshiO|ja|風木津別之忍男神|12=draft|12=draft}}'''. Regarding the reading of the name, the original text notes that the character for "木" should be read as "mo." However, the ''Kojikiden'' suggests this annotation was mistakenly inserted later, proposing the reading "Kazaketsuwake Oshio." The ''Kojikiden'' also considers this deity to be the same as {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|ja|底筒男神|en|Sumiyoshi sanjin=Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Sokotsutsuo-no-Kami|12=draft|12=draft}} or {{ill|Hayasasurahime|ja|速佐須良比売|12=draft|12=draft}}, who appears in the ''Ōharae no Kotoba''.
== See Also == * [[List of Japanese deities]] * [[Kamiumi]]

Regarding the reading of the name, the original text notes that the character for "木" should be read as "mo." However, the ''Kojikiden'' suggests this annotation was mistakenly inserted later, proposing the reading "Kazaketsuwake Oshio." The ''Kojikiden'' also considers this deity to be the same as {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|ja|底筒男神|en|Sumiyoshi sanjin=Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Sokotsutsuo-no-Kami|12=draft|12=draft}} or {{ill|Hayasasurahime|ja|速佐須良比売|12=draft|12=draft}}, who appears in the ''Ōharae no Kotoba''.

[[Image:Descendants of Izanagi and Izanami.svg|thumb|right|250px|Myth of the Birth of the Gods (Deities Born from Izanagi and Izanami) [[Media:Descendants of Izanagi and Izanami.svg|View in SVG (for supported browsers)]]]] '''Katakurokushin''' (家宅六神) refers to the collective name of the six [[deities (Shinto)|deities]] representing (or protecting) [[households]] in [[Shinto]].
The Katakurokushin represent the materials and structure of buildings. According to the ''[[Kojiki]]'', all six deities were born after the [[Kuniumi]] (birth of the land), following the birth of '''{{ill|Ōkoto-oshi'o|ja|大事忍男神|12=draft|12=draft}}''', as children of {{ill|Izanagi|ja|イザナギ|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Izanami|ja|イザナミ|12=draft|12=draft}}.
== Iwatsuchi-biko-no-Kami == The first deity born is '''{{ill|Iwatsuchi-BiKo|ja|石土毘古神|12=draft|12=draft}}'''.
== Iwasu-hime-no-Kami == The second deity is '''{{ill|Iwasu-HiMe|ja|石巣比売神|12=draft|12=draft}}'''.
According to the {{ill|Koshiden|ja|古史伝|12=draft|12=draft}}, these two deities are alternative names for {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|ja|上筒男神|Sumiyoshi sanjin=Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Uwatatsu-no-Kami|12=draft|12=draft}}. In {{ill|Jinmyōkō|8=ja|9=神名考|12=draft|12=draft}}, it is stated that Iwatsuchi-biko-no-Kami governs over the [[earth]], while Iwasu-hime-no-Kami governs over [[:en:Sand|sand]].
== Ōtohiwake-no-Kami == The third deity is '''{{ill|Ōtohi-WaKe|ja|大戸日別神|12=draft|12=draft}}''', whose gender is unknown. The ''[[Kojikiden]]'' suggests that this deity may be confused with {{ill|Ōna-obi|ja|大直毘神|12=draft|12=draft}}. In ''Jinmyōkō'', Ōtohiwake-no-Kami is considered one of the gate deities.
== Ame-no-Fukio-no-Kami == The fourth deity is '''{{ill|Ame-no-FukiO|ja|天之吹男神|12=draft|12=draft}}'''. The ''Kojikiden'' interprets this deity, based on the name, as being the same as {{ill|Ibukidōnushi|ja|気吹戸主|12=draft|12=draft}}, who appears in the ''[[Oharae no Kotoba|Ōharae no Kotoba]]''. In ''Jinmyōkō'', this deity is considered the deity of rooftops.
== Oyabiko-no-Kami == The fifth deity is '''{{ill|Oyabiko|ja|大屋毘古神|12=draft|12=draft}}'''. This is considered a different deity from the {{ill|Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|ja|大屋毘古神|lt=Oyabiko-no-Kami|12=draft|12=draft}} who appears in the myths of {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主|12=draft|12=draft}} and is also an alternative name for {{ill|Itonokami|ja|五十猛神|12=draft|12=draft}}.
== Kazamo-tsuwake-no-Oshio-no-Kami == The last deity born is '''{{ill|Kazamo-Tsuwake-no-OshiO|ja|風木津別之忍男神|12=draft|12=draft}}'''. Regarding the reading of the name, the original text notes that the character for "木" should be read as "mo." However, the ''Kojikiden'' suggests this annotation was mistakenly inserted later, proposing the reading "Kazaketsuwake Oshio." The ''Kojikiden'' also considers this deity to be the same as {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|ja|底筒男神|en|Sumiyoshi sanjin=Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Sokotsutsuo-no-Kami|12=draft|12=draft}} or {{ill|Hayasasurahime|ja|速佐須良比売|12=draft|12=draft}}, who appears in the ''Ōharae no Kotoba''.
